qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary credential use via whitelisted TTS endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: No match: this finding relies on /api/v1/text-to-speech/generate being whitelisted and on direct credentialId use, while the advisory root cause is the authenticated /api/v1 middleware branch that accepts x-request-from: internal.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated PII exposure via whitelisted leads endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: No match: this finding is an unauthenticated WHITELIST_URLS exposure of GET /api/v1/leads/:id, not the advisory's spoofed internal-header authorization bypass for authenticated tenant sessions.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is specifically the Flowise packages/server/src/index.ts /api/v1 middleware short-circuit that trusts x-request-from: internal for authenticated sessions; the manifest-listed qualifying findings concern endpoints made unauthenticated through WHITELIST_URLS.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-wvhq-wp8g-c7vq describes packages/server/src/index.ts middleware bypassing /api/v1 authorization when a client supplies x-request-from: internal with a valid UI session cookie.

unauth-credential-use-tts/vulnerability_report.json reports a whitelisted /api/v1/text-to-speech/generate endpoint and credentialId lookup without ownership checks, not the spoofed internal-header branch.
unauth-leaks-pii/vulnerability_report.json reports a whitelisted /api/v1/leads/:id endpoint returning lead records without authentication, not the spoofed internal-header branch.
